site.title: Katherine Reads
site.tagline: The 100 greatest books of all time, ranked.
site.about: Welcome to the 100 Greatest Books of All Time list. New thoughts and reviews go up on my Instagram, @katherinereads100. Happy reading!

tiers.note: Fill in the `tier` field for each book with one of: S, A, B, C, D, E, F. Leave empty string for unranked.

cover_note: cover_url uses the Open Library cover-by-search pattern. If a cover is missing or wrong, replace the URL. When you move to Amazon affiliate, swap to Amazon product images.
link_note: amazon_link is an Amazon search URL (reliable placeholder). Replace with exact product links or affiliate links later.

books.title: Nineteen Eighty-Four
books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A chilling vision of total surveillance where even your thoughts aren't your own.

books.title: A Tale of Two Cities
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.description: Love and sacrifice set against the blood and fervor of the French Revolution.

books.title: Dune
books.author: Frank Herbert
books.description: A desert planet, a prophesied heir, and the most epic worldbuilding in science fiction.

books.title: The Secret Garden
books.author: Frances Hodgson Burnett
books.description: A lonely girl, a hidden garden, and the quiet magic of things coming back to life.

books.title: Pride and Prejudice
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: The sharpest, most satisfying enemies-to-lovers romance ever written.

books.title: The Handmaid's Tale
books.author: Margaret Atwood
books.description: A terrifyingly plausible future where women are property of the state.

books.title: Fahrenheit 451
books.author: Ray Bradbury
books.description: A fireman who burns books begins to wonder what they're worth saving.

books.title: Animal Farm
books.author: George Orwell
books.description: Farm animals overthrow their owner, then learn power corrupts all over again.

books.title: The Master and Margarita
books.author: Mikhail Bulgakov
books.description: The Devil visits Soviet Moscow in a wild, funny, unclassifiable masterpiece.

books.title: The Iliad
books.author: Homer
books.description: Rage, glory, and grief on the killing fields of Troy.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë
books.description: A plain, fierce governess refuses to bend, even for the man she loves.

books.title: East of Eden
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.description: A sprawling family saga about good, evil, and the freedom to choose.

books.title: The Odyssey
books.author: Homer
books.description: The original epic road trip: one man's ten-year struggle to get home.

books.title: The Picture of Dorian Gray
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.description: A beautiful young man stays flawless while his portrait rots with his sins.

books.title: Adventures of Huckleberry Finn
books.author: Mark Twain
books.description: A runaway boy and an escaping slave raft down the Mississippi toward freedom.

books.title: The Fountainhead
books.author: Ayn Rand
books.description: An uncompromising architect would rather destroy his work than betray his vision.

books.title: Darkness at Noon
books.author: Arthur Koestler
books.description: An old revolutionary is jailed by the regime he helped create.

books.title: The Fellowship of the Ring
books.author: J. R. R. Tolkien
books.description: A small hobbit shoulders a terrible ring and the fate of the world.

books.title: Lord of the Flies
books.author: William Golding
books.description: Stranded schoolboys discover how thin the veneer of civilization really is.

books.title: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
books.author: James Joyce
books.description: A young man breaks from faith and family to become an artist.

books.title: Rebecca
books.author: Daphne du Maurier
books.description: A new bride is haunted by the perfect dead wife she can't compete with.

books.title: Great Expectations
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.description: An orphan's mysterious fortune teaches him what really makes a gentleman.

books.title: Don Quixote
books.author: Miguel de Cervantes
books.description: An old man reads too many chivalry novels and rides off to be a knight.

books.title: The Sun Also Rises
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.description: Lost-generation expats drift through Paris and Pamplona, wounded and searching.

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.author: Chinua Achebe
books.description: A proud Igbo leader watches colonialism unravel everything he knows.

books.title: Invisible Man
books.author: Ralph Ellison
books.description: A Black man in mid-century America finds himself unseen by everyone around him.

books.title: I, Claudius
books.author: Robert Graves
books.description: A stammering, overlooked prince narrates the murderous rise of imperial Rome.

books.title: Slaughterhouse-Five
books.author: Kurt Vonnegut
books.description: A WWII soldier comes unstuck in time after surviving the firebombing of Dresden.

books.title: To the Lighthouse
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.description: A family, a summer house, and the quiet ache of time passing.

books.title: Ulysses
books.author: James Joyce
books.description: One ordinary Dublin day rendered in the most ambitious prose ever attempted.

books.title: Middlemarch
books.author: George Eliot
books.description: An entire English town's worth of ambition, marriage, and disappointment.

books.title: Les Misérables
books.author: Victor Hugo
books.description: An ex-convict's lifelong bid for redemption, hunted every step of the way.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: A small-town lawyer defends an innocent Black man through his daughter's eyes.

books.title: Memoirs of a Geisha
books.author: Arthur Golden
books.description: A poor girl is sold into the secret, glittering world of pre-war Kyoto.

books.title: Mrs Dalloway
books.author: Virginia Woolf
books.description: A society hostess plans a party while a whole inner life unspools beneath it.

books.title: The Prime of Miss Jean Brodie
books.author: Muriel Spark
books.description: A charismatic teacher shapes her favorite girls, for better and for worse.

books.title: Of Human Bondage
books.author: W. Somerset Maugham
books.description: A sensitive young man is consumed by a love he knows is destroying him.

books.title: Tropic of Cancer
books.author: Henry Miller
books.description: A broke writer's raw, scandalous account of bohemian life in Paris.

books.title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
books.author: Zora Neale Hurston
books.description: A Black woman in the rural South searches three marriages for her own voice.

books.title: On the Road
books.author: Jack Kerouac
books.description: The restless, jazz-fueled bible of the Beat generation's wanderlust.

books.title: The Death of the Heart
books.author: Elizabeth Bowen
books.description: A naive orphan's diary exposes the cruelties of a polished London household.

books.title: Brideshead Revisited
books.author: Evelyn Waugh
books.description: An outsider is enchanted, then heartbroken, by a fading aristocratic family.

books.title: Wide Sargasso Sea
books.author: Jean Rhys
books.description: The untold story of the madwoman in Jane Eyre's attic.

books.title: Portnoy's Complaint
books.author: Philip Roth
books.description: A neurotic man unloads his guilt, lust, and family on his therapist.

books.title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
books.author: Ken Kesey
books.description: A free-spirited rebel takes on a tyrannical nurse in a mental ward.

books.title: And Then There Were None
books.author: Agatha Christie
books.description: Ten strangers on an island are murdered one by one. The best whodunit ever.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.description: A scientist creates life, then abandons it, in the first true science fiction novel.

books.title: The Little Prince
books.author: Antoine de Saint-Exupéry
books.description: A pilot meets a small prince who teaches him to see with the heart.

books.title: As I Lay Dying
books.author: William Faulkner
books.description: A poor family hauls their mother's coffin across Mississippi, told in fractured voices.

books.title: The Godfather
books.author: Mario Puzo
books.description: The rise of a mafia dynasty and the son who never wanted in.

books.title: The Wind-Up Bird Chronicle
books.author: Haruki Murakami
books.description: A man's search for his missing cat spirals into something strange and dreamlike.

books.title: The Sound and the Fury
books.author: William Faulkner
books.description: A Southern family's decline told through four wildly different minds.

books.title: The Stranger
books.author: Albert Camus
books.description: A man kills a stranger and feels nothing, in the defining novel of the absurd.

books.title: Beloved
books.author: Toni Morrison
books.description: A formerly enslaved mother is haunted, literally, by the daughter she lost.

books.title: The Scarlet Letter
books.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
books.description: A woman wears her sin in public while the man who shared it stays hidden.

books.title: A Confederacy of Dunces
books.author: John Kennedy Toole
books.description: A pompous, lazy genius wreaks comic havoc across New Orleans.

books.title: War and Peace
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.description: Russian aristocrats love, fight, and grow up against the Napoleonic Wars.

books.title: Bleak House
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.description: An endless lawsuit poisons everyone it touches in Dickens's sharpest novel.

books.title: The Trial
books.author: Franz Kafka
books.description: A man is arrested and prosecuted by a faceless system that never names his crime.

books.title: Gulliver's Travels
books.author: Jonathan Swift
books.description: A traveler's tall tales double as a savage satire of human folly.

books.title: American Pastoral
books.author: Philip Roth
books.description: A golden all-American life shatters when a man's daughter turns radical.

books.title: The Left Hand of Darkness
books.author: Ursula K. Le Guin
books.description: An envoy to a genderless world must rethink everything he knows about people.

books.title: A Farewell to Arms
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.description: An ambulance driver and a nurse fall in love amid the ruin of WWI.

books.title: Dracula
books.author: Bram Stoker
books.description: The count comes to London, and the vampire legend is born.

books.title: The Secret History
books.author: Donna Tartt
books.description: A clique of classics students commits murder, then comes apart at the seams.

books.title: A House for Mr. Biswas
books.author: V. S. Naipaul
books.description: One man's lifelong struggle to own a home, and a scrap of dignity, in Trinidad.

books.title: Go Tell It on the Mountain
books.author: James Baldwin
books.description: A Harlem boy wrestles with faith, family, and his stepfather's wrath.

books.title: A Bend in the River
books.author: V. S. Naipaul
books.description: A shopkeeper tries to build a life in a newly independent African nation.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A father's murder forces three brothers to confront God, doubt, and guilt.

books.title: Perfume
books.author: Patrick Süskind
books.description: A man with no scent of his own murders to capture the perfect human smell.

books.title: A Prayer for Owen Meany
books.author: John Irving
books.description: A tiny boy with a wrecked voice believes he's God's instrument, and may be right.

books.title: The Road
books.author: Cormac McCarthy
books.description: A father and son walk a dead world, carrying nothing but each other.

books.title: The Color Purple
books.author: Alice Walker
books.description: A Black woman in the South finds her voice through letters and hard-won love.

books.title: A Game of Thrones
books.author: George R. R. Martin
books.description: Noble houses scheme and slaughter for a throne while winter creeps closer.

books.title: The Pillars of the Earth
books.author: Ken Follett
books.description: The building of a cathedral becomes an epic of ambition, faith, and feud.

books.title: Scoop
books.author: Evelyn Waugh
books.description: A mild nature columnist is mistaken for a war correspondent, with farcical results.

books.title: Sense and Sensibility
books.author: Jane Austen
books.description: Two sisters, one all reason and one all feeling, both learn the cost of love.

books.title: All the King's Men
books.author: Robert Penn Warren
books.description: A populist politician's rise and corruption, seen by the man who enables him.

books.title: The Age of Innocence
books.author: Edith Wharton
books.description: A man engaged to the perfect woman falls for the one society forbids.

books.title: Wuthering Heights
books.author: Emily Brontë
books.description: A love so fierce it curses two families across the moors for generations.

books.title: Heart of Darkness
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.description: A river journey into the Congo becomes a descent into human darkness.

books.title: The Wind in the Willows
books.author: Kenneth Grahame
books.description: Mole, Rat, and the reckless Mr. Toad in the coziest of riverbank adventures.

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas
books.description: A wronged man escapes prison, finds a fortune, and engineers perfect revenge.

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.description: Four sisters grow up, chase dreams, and break your heart in Civil War New England.

books.title: The Call of the Wild
books.author: Jack London
books.description: A domestic dog is dragged to the Yukon and rediscovers his wild instincts.

books.title: A Clockwork Orange
books.author: Anthony Burgess
books.description: A violent young thug is brainwashed into goodness, raising the question of free will.

books.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
books.author: Lewis Carroll
books.description: A girl falls down a rabbit hole into the most gloriously nonsensical world.

books.title: Anna Karenina
books.author: Leo Tolstoy
books.description: A married woman risks everything for passion in the great novel of love and ruin.

books.title: Catch-22
books.author: Joseph Heller
books.description: A WWII bombardier tries to stay sane inside the insane logic of war.

books.title: Brave New World
books.author: Aldous Huxley
books.description: A pleasure-engineered society where happiness has quietly replaced freedom.

books.title: A Passage to India
books.author: E. M. Forster
books.description: An ambiguous incident in a cave ignites the tensions of colonial India.

books.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
books.author: Douglas Adams
books.description: Earth is demolished for a highway, and the funniest sci-fi romp begins.

books.title: Crime and Punishment
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.description: A student murders for a theory, then drowns in guilt and paranoia.

books.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
books.author: Gabriel García Márquez
books.description: Seven generations of one family in a town where the magical is everyday.

books.title: Moby-Dick
books.author: Herman Melville
books.description: A captain's obsession with one white whale drags his whole crew toward doom.

books.title: Lolita
books.author: Vladimir Nabokov
books.description: A predator's beautiful, monstrous confession, told in dazzling, damning prose.

books.title: The Catcher in the Rye
books.author: J. D. Salinger
books.description: A disaffected teen wanders New York, raging against everything phony.

books.title: The Grapes of Wrath
books.author: John Steinbeck
books.description: A dust-bowl family heads west for work and finds the American dream broken.

books.title: The Hobbit
books.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
books.description: A comfortable hobbit is swept into a quest for treasure and a sleeping dragon.

books.title: The Shining
books.author: Stephen King
books.description: A haunted hotel slowly turns a struggling father into his family's nightmare.
